revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja, Okayama| Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja, Okayama]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]]

There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. {{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).

The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.

The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers

{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}

It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the Kofun can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the Kofun can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the Kofun can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the Kofun can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}

It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[breccia]] and rows of ''[[haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}

Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.

revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[Sōja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[:en:Breccia|Breccia]] and rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja, Okayama]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
[[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)]]

revision.text:
{{For-text|the Tsukuriyama Kofun in Okayama city|[[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun | native_name =作山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofun, Soja air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sōja|Sōja, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|51.18|N|133|46|8.52|E}}}}}} [[file:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja), zenkei.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Panoramic view]] {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun'''|作山古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Misu neighborhood of [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, orientated to the southwest. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[:en:Breccia|Breccia]] and rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[:en:San'yōdō|San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads. The Tsukuriyama Kofun is cleared of undergrowth in December, so the shape of the burial mound can be clearly seen around this time.
;Total length: 282 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 170 meters wide x 23 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 175 x 160 meters diameter x 24 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Soja)}} *[https://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/kuni/kuni_07.html Soja City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/681583.html Okayama Prefectural Ancient Kibi Cultural Properties Center home page]{{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja, Okayama]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206213|title=山古墳 第一古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the second largest tumulus in Okayama Prefecture and the tenth largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 5th century (in the middle of the Kofun period).

The tumulus was constructed in three tiers and has a total length of 282 meters. The outer surface has ''[[fukiishi]]'' of [[:en:Breccia|Breccia]] and rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. No trace of a surrounding moat has been found. The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown; however, it is presumed to be intact, since no indication of robbery pits have been found. Most of the items unearthed from the Tsukuriyama Kofun are cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'', and the total number is estimated to be as many as 5000. It is estimated that these were made in the second quarter of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ancient [[:en:San'yōdō|San'yōdō]] highway passes through on south side of the tumulus, and it is speculated that the site was selected to show off the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Tsukuriyama Kofun has a number of unusual aspects to its design. The mound incorporated a natural hill, some portions of which remain intact, especially towards the anterior portion. There is also a trapezoidal protrusion at the front edge of the anterior part. The circular posterior mound is oval instead of circular, perhaps to make it look larger. The northern part of the tumulus remains in good condition, but the southern part has undergone major alterations due to the construction of private houses and roads.
